stem: For the ellipse \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}+\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the focal distance \( c \).
For the ellipse \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}+\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the eccentricity \( e \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by \( b \) (the semi-minor axis) instead of \( a \).
This uses \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2-b^2}\).
This reports the axis ratio \( b/a \) instead of the eccentricity.
This forgets to take the square root of \( a^2-b^2\) before dividing.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct eccentricity.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct eccentricity.

stem: For the ellipse \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}+\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}=1 \) (major axis vertical, since \( [[a]]>[[b]] \)), find the \( y \)-coordinate of the focus that lies above the center.
solution: Since the larger denominator \( [[a]]^{2}\) is under \( y^{2}\), the major axis is vertical and the foci are at \( (0,\pm c) \) with \( c=\sqrt{a^{2}-b^{2}}=[[answer]] \); the one above the center has \( y=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This takes the square root the wrong way, as if \( b\) were the semi-major axis.
This uses \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2-b^2}\).
This subtracts without squaring first.
This gives the focus below the center instead of above it.
This is off by one from the correct coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct coordinate.

stem: For the ellipse \( \dfrac{(x-[[h]])^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}+\dfrac{(y-[[k]])^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the \( x \)-coordinate of the focus to the right of the center.
solution: The center is \( ([[h]],[[k]]) \) and \( c=\sqrt{a^{2}-b^{2}} \), so the right focus has \( x=[[h]]+c=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts \( c\) instead of adding it, giving the left focus.
This forgets to shift by the center's \( x\)-coordinate \( h\).
This uses \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2-b^2}\).
This shifts by \( k\) instead of \( h\).
This is off by one from the correct coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct coordinate.

stem: An ellipse has semi-major axis \( [[a]] \). A point \( P \) on the ellipse is at distance \( [[d1]] \) from one focus. What is its distance from the other focus?
solution: By the definition of an ellipse, the two focal distances always sum to \( 2a \), so the other distance is \( 2([[a]])-[[d1]]=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the distances instead of using the constant-sum property.
This uses \( a\) instead of \( 2a\) as the constant sum.
This computes the sum minus double the semi-major axis, giving a negative, incorrect result.
This uses \( a/2\) instead of \( 2a\) as the total.
This is off by one from the correct distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct distance.

stem: The ellipse \( [[p]]x^{2}+[[q]]y^{2}=1 \) is not yet in standard form \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}+\dfrac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \). Find its eccentricity.
solution: Dividing by \( 1 \) directly, \( a^{2}=\dfrac{1}{[[p]]} \) and \( b^{2}=\dfrac{1}{[[q]]} \) (with \( a^2>b^2\) since \( [[p]]<[[q]] \)), so \( e=\sqrt{1-\dfrac{b^{2}}{a^{2}}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{[[q]]-[[p]]}{[[q]]}}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( p/q\) under the root instead of \( (q-p)/q\).
This divides by \( p\) instead of \( q\) in the final ratio.
This forgets to take the square root.
This adds \( p\) and \( q\) instead of subtracting.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct eccentricity.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct eccentricity.

stem: Find the area enclosed by the ellipse \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}+\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \).
solution: The area of an ellipse with semi-axes \( a,b \) is \( \pi ab=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \pi a^2\) (a circle's formula) instead of \( \pi ab\).
This uses \( \pi(a+b)\) instead of \( \pi ab\).
This doubles the correct area.
This halves the correct area.
This is off by one from the correct area.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct area.

stem: An ellipse has semi-major axis \( a=[[a]] \) and eccentricity \( e=\dfrac{[[m]]}{[[n]]} \). Find the focal distance \( c \).
solution: Since \( e=\dfrac{c}{a} \), the focal distance is \( c=ea=[[a]]\cdot\dfrac{[[m]]}{[[n]]}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the eccentricity fraction before multiplying.
This divides by \( e\) instead of multiplying by it.
This adds \( e\) to \( a\) instead of multiplying.
This computes \( b=a-c\)-style subtraction instead of \( c=ea\).
This is off by one from the correct focal distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct focal distance.
